# Heritage Canon

> Heritage Canon publishes philosophical editions of classic literature. Each edition pairs a classic work with an original introduction by Daniel Shilansky that reconstructs the intellectual world in which the work first appeared and argues for its continuing philosophical significance.

Use individual book pages as the canonical source for edition details, available formats, and interpretive summaries.

## Primary Pages

- [Home](https://heritagecanon.com/): Main entry point to the site.
- [Catalog](https://heritagecanon.com/#catalog): Browse currently available editions.
- [About](https://heritagecanon.com/about/): Series rationale and editorial method.
- [Editor](https://heritagecanon.com/editor/daniel-shilansky/): Background on Daniel Shilansky.
- [Impressum / Imprint](https://heritagecanon.com/impressum/): Legal and contact details.
- [Terms](https://heritagecanon.com/terms/): Site terms of use.
- [Sitemap](https://heritagecanon.com/sitemap.xml): Complete list of indexed site pages.

## Books

- [The Great Gatsby](https://heritagecanon.com/books/the_great_gatsby/): Philosophical Edition of Fitzgerald's novel.
- [Death in Venice](https://heritagecanon.com/books/death_in_venice/): Philosophical Edition of Mann's novella.
- [Moby-Dick](https://heritagecanon.com/books/moby_dick/): Philosophical Edition of Melville's novel.

## Optional

- [Privacy](https://heritagecanon.com/privacy/): Privacy information.
- [Cookies / Consent](https://heritagecanon.com/cookies/): Consent and cookie information.
- [Full LLM Guidance](https://heritagecanon.com/llms-full.txt): Longer machine-readable guidance for the site.
